message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="9/9 consensus reached — finalize documents now">
We have 9/9 consensus! All reviewers have approved. Kent updated his position to approve all 8 stories as Must Have. 

Please finalize the documents now — incorporate the agreed feedback (Heydon's a11y criteria, oldest-first inbox, 5 default contexts, header nav, clarify flow details, US-GTD-2 split). Once you've made the final updates, let me know so I can present PROJECT.md to the project owner for scope approval, then we commit and move to implementation.
</teammate-message>

355→The product is evolving from a simple todo list into a full GTD system. These stories
   356→define the V1 GTD scope. See `docs/gtd-product-discovery.md` for the complete product
   357→discovery document.
   358→
   359→### US-GTD-1: Capture to Inbox
   360→
   361→**As a** GTD practitioner,
   362→**I want to** quickly capture anything on my mind into my inbox,
   363→**So that** I can get it out of my head and process it later.
   364→
   365→#### Acceptance Criteria
   366→
   367→- [ ] Inbox capture input is always accessible (prominent on the main page, and available
   368→  via a persistent capture input on every authenticated page)
   369→- [ ] Capture is fast: type and press Enter, item appears in inbox
   370→- [ ] No categorization required at capture time (that happens during clarify)
   371→- [ ] Inbox items show in a list ordered by capture time (oldest first -- process in
   372→  FIFO order, like a physical inbox)
   373→- [ ] Inbox count is visible in the navigation so users know how many items need processing
   374→- [ ] Input has a visible `<label>` and helpful placeholder text
   375→- [ ] After capturing, the input field is cleared and focus returns to it
   376→- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only submissions are silently ignored
   377→- [ ] Title has a maximum length of 300 characters
   378→- [ ] Works without JavaScript (full page reload); HTMX enhances
   379→- [ ] `aria-live` region announces "Captured to inbox" after HTMX swap
   380→- [ ] Capture event is logged with structured tracing
   381→
   382→#### Accessibility
   383→- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
   384→- [ ] Capture input has a visible `<label>` associated via `for`/`id`
   385→- [ ] Focus management: after capture, focus returns to the input
   386→- [ ] Skip link bypasses header capture input to reach main content
   387→- [ ] Screen reader announces inbox count change after capture
   388→
   389→#### Edge Cases
   390→- Adding with only whitespace (silently ignored)
   391→- Rapid successive captures
   392→- Very long text (at or beyond max length)
   393→- Special characters, HTML, emoji
   394→
   395→#### Success Metric
   396→Capturing a thought takes under 3 seconds from thought to inbox.
   397→
   398→---
   399→
   400→### US-GTD-2: Clarify Inbox Items
   401→
   402→**As a** GTD practitioner,
   403→**I want to** process each inbox item through the GTD clarify workflow,
   404→**So that** I can decide what each item means and where it belongs.

file.content:
The product is evolving from a simple todo list into a full GTD system. These stories
define the V1 GTD scope. See `docs/gtd-product-discovery.md` for the complete product
discovery document.

### US-GTD-1: Capture to Inbox

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** quickly capture anything on my mind into my inbox,
**So that** I can get it out of my head and process it later.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Inbox capture input is always accessible (prominent on the main page, and available
  via a persistent capture input on every authenticated page)
- [ ] Capture is fast: type and press Enter, item appears in inbox
- [ ] No categorization required at capture time (that happens during clarify)
- [ ] Inbox items show in a list ordered by capture time (oldest first -- process in
  FIFO order, like a physical inbox)
- [ ] Inbox count is visible in the navigation so users know how many items need processing
- [ ] Input has a visible `<label>` and helpful placeholder text
- [ ] After capturing, the input field is cleared and focus returns to it
- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only submissions are silently ignored
- [ ] Title has a maximum length of 300 characters
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (full page reload); HTMX enhances
- [ ] `aria-live` region announces "Captured to inbox" after HTMX swap
- [ ] Capture event is logged with structured tracing

#### Accessibility
- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
- [ ] Capture input has a visible `<label>` associated via `for`/`id`
- [ ] Focus management: after capture, focus returns to the input
- [ ] Skip link bypasses header capture input to reach main content
- [ ] Screen reader announces inbox count change after capture

#### Edge Cases
- Adding with only whitespace (silently ignored)
- Rapid successive captures
- Very long text (at or beyond max length)
- Special characters, HTML, emoji

#### Success Metric
Capturing a thought takes under 3 seconds from thought to inbox.

---

### US-GTD-2: Clarify Inbox Items

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** process each inbox item through the GTD clarify workflow,
**So that** I can decide what each item means and where it belongs.

## 1. The User Problem

83→## V1 Scope: Getting Things Done (GTD) System
    84→
    85→The product is evolving from a simple todo list into a full GTD (Getting Things Done)
    86→system. See `docs/gtd-product-discovery.md` for the complete product discovery document.
    87→
    88→### Must Have
    89→- **Inbox capture**: Quick capture of anything on the user's mind, minimal friction
    90→- **Clarify workflow**: Process inbox items through GTD decision tree (actionable?
    91→  next action? project? delegate? defer? trash?)
    92→- **Next Actions list with contexts**: Actions organized by context (@computer, @home,
    93→  @errands, etc.) so users work from context-appropriate lists
    94→- **Projects list**: Track multi-step outcomes; each project has at least one next action;
    95→  stalled projects are flagged
    96→- **Waiting For list**: Track delegated/blocked items with who/what and date
    97→- **Someday/Maybe list**: Park ideas without cluttering action lists
    98→- **Weekly Review guided flow**: Three-phase review (Get Clear, Get Current, Get Creative)
    99→  to keep the system trustworthy
   100→- **GTD navigation and dashboard**: Navigate between all GTD lists; dashboard shows
   101→  inbox count, stalled projects, action counts
   102→- **Complete and delete**: Mark actions done, remove irrelevant items across all lists
   103→
   104→### Should Have
   105→- Project support materials (notes attached to projects)
   106→- Move items between lists (reclassify as understanding changes)
   107→
   108→### Could Have
   109→- Review scheduling (remind when weekly review is due)
   110→- Bulk inbox processing
   111→
   112→### Out of Scope (Future)
   113→- Calendar / date-specific items (requires date picker, time zones, recurring events)
   114→- Reference file storage (needs file upload, search, storage infrastructure)
   115→- Horizons of Focus / goals / vision (higher-level planning)
   116→- Natural language input ("call Bob tomorrow")
   117→- Email-to-inbox capture (requires email infrastructure)
   118→- Mobile native app (responsive web is sufficient)
   119→- Sharing / delegation to other users (multi-user collaboration)
   120→- Tags beyond contexts
   121→- Search and filter
   122→- Dark mode
   123→- Drag-and-drop reordering
   124→- Recurring items
   125→- Social login (OAuth)
   126→

file.content:
## V1 Scope: Getting Things Done (GTD) System

The product is evolving from a simple todo list into a full GTD (Getting Things Done)
system. See `docs/gtd-product-discovery.md` for the complete product discovery document.

### Must Have
- **Inbox capture**: Quick capture of anything on the user's mind, minimal friction
- **Clarify workflow**: Process inbox items through GTD decision tree (actionable?
  next action? project? delegate? defer? trash?)
- **Next Actions list with contexts**: Actions organized by context (@computer, @home,
  @errands, etc.) so users work from context-appropriate lists
- **Projects list**: Track multi-step outcomes; each project has at least one next action;
  stalled projects are flagged
- **Waiting For list**: Track delegated/blocked items with who/what and date
- **Someday/Maybe list**: Park ideas without cluttering action lists
- **Weekly Review guided flow**: Three-phase review (Get Clear, Get Current, Get Creative)
  to keep the system trustworthy
- **GTD navigation and dashboard**: Navigate between all GTD lists; dashboard shows
  inbox count, stalled projects, action counts
- **Complete and delete**: Mark actions done, remove irrelevant items across all lists

### Should Have
- Project support materials (notes attached to projects)
- Move items between lists (reclassify as understanding changes)

### Could Have
- Review scheduling (remind when weekly review is due)
- Bulk inbox processing

### Out of Scope (Future)
- Calendar / date-specific items (requires date picker, time zones, recurring events)
- Reference file storage (needs file upload, search, storage infrastructure)
- Horizons of Focus / goals / vision (higher-level planning)
- Natural language input ("call Bob tomorrow")
- Email-to-inbox capture (requires email infrastructure)
- Mobile native app (responsive web is sufficient)
- Sharing / delegation to other users (multi-user collaboration)
- Tags beyond contexts
- Search and filter
- Dark mode
- Drag-and-drop reordering
- Recurring items
- Social login (OAuth)

content.text: Everything looks good. All changes are already in place. Let me confirm to the team lead.

input.content:
All feedback was already incorporated in my previous edit round. I've just verified all documents are clean and consistent. Here's the final state:

## Verified Final State

**`docs/gtd-product-discovery.md`**:
- Status: "APPROVED -- team consensus reached (9/9)"
- Default contexts: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere (5, no @office)
- Navigation: header nav (not sidebar)
- Implementation order: 13 steps with interleaved clarify sub-stories
- Open Questions replaced with "Resolved Decisions" (7 decisions documented)

**`docs/user-stories.md`**:
- 8 GTD user stories, each with dedicated Accessibility section (Heydon's criteria)
- US-GTD-1: oldest-first inbox ordering
- US-GTD-2: clarify interaction pattern (single form, radio buttons, progressive disclosure), split into sub-stories 2a-2e
- US-GTD-8: duplicate capture input note for Inbox page
- Implementation order: 13 steps matching discovery doc

**`docs/glossary.md`**:
- Status: "Approved" (not "Proposed")
- Default contexts updated to 5

**`PROJECT.md`**:
- V1 GTD scope with Must Have / Should Have / Could Have / Out of Scope
- Two-minute rule dropped from Could Have
- Custom contexts and inbox badge are implicit in Must Have

**`docs/future-ideas.md`**:
- Updated for V1 GTD scope

Documents are finalized and ready. Please present PROJECT.md to the project owner for scope approval. Once approved, I'm ready to commit.
input.summary: Documents finalized and verified, ready for owner approval
